raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "\"Check this\" admits logical consistency review as a primary reading; the artifact contains several undefended inferential moves — most notably the claim that the 2019 five-class taxonomy 'aligns more naturally' with ALERT-Bench's evaluation grid and the implicit equivalence between held-out Rubin commissioning data and future LSST performance.",
    "R1.3": "Multiple quantitative claims (1.4× cost-adjusted F1, 2.4M ZTF alerts, routing-cost metric definition) are stated without reference to any supporting figure, table, or appendix; alignment between claim and evidence must be flagged as structurally unverifiable in this excerpt.",
    "R1.6": "Prose review is the modal interpretation of a bare 'check this' on a written artifact; sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, and register consistency are in scope.",
    "R1.7": "The artifact's validity leans heavily on two Carrasco-Davis et al. citations (2019, 2021); accuracy of years, scope attribution, and adequacy of related-work coverage (e.g., ALeRCE broker, ParSNIP, Fink) is directly checkable and materially affects novelty framing."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on factual and citation accuracy — verifying that Carrasco-Davis et al. (2019/2021) reported the scope and numbers described, and that related-work coverage is adequate (R1.3, R1.7) — or on writing quality and logical consistency — identifying unsupported assertions, unclear prose, and internal contradictions (R1.2, R1.6)?"
}

rationale.R1.2: "Check this" admits logical consistency review as a primary reading; the artifact contains several undefended inferential moves — most notably the claim that the 2019 five-class taxonomy 'aligns more naturally' with ALERT-Bench's evaluation grid and the implicit equivalence between held-out Rubin commissioning data and future LSST performance.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple quantitative claims (1.4× cost-adjusted F1, 2.4M ZTF alerts, routing-cost metric definition) are stated without reference to any supporting figure, table, or appendix; alignment between claim and evidence must be flagged as structurally unverifiable in this excerpt.
rationale.R1.6: Prose review is the modal interpretation of a bare 'check this' on a written artifact; sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, and register consistency are in scope.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact's validity leans heavily on two Carrasco-Davis et al. citations (2019, 2021); accuracy of years, scope attribution, and adequacy of related-work coverage (e.g., ALeRCE broker, ParSNIP, Fink) is directly checkable and materially affects novelty framing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on factual and citation accuracy — verifying that Carrasco-Davis et al. (2019/2021) reported the scope and numbers described, and that related-work coverage is adequate (R1.3, R1.7) — or on writing quality and logical consistency — identifying unsupported assertions, unclear prose, and internal contradictions (R1.2, R1.6)?
